note_data.title: Circular Motion and Gravitation
keyPoints: Circular motion is the motion of a body in a circular path with constant speed but changing velocity due to continuous change in direction
Centripetal force is always directed towards the center of circular path and is necessary to keep a body in circular motion
Angular velocity is the rate of change of angular displacement and is measured in radians per second
Newton's law of universal gravitation states that every particle in the universe attracts every other particle with a force directly proportional to the product of their masses
Weight of a body is the gravitational force acting on it and varies with location, while mass remains constant
Acceleration due to gravity decreases with height above Earth's surface and varies at different locations
Satellites revolve around Earth due to gravitational force which provides the required centripetal force
Tidal forces are caused by gravitational attraction of moon and sun on Earth's oceans

definitions.definition: The motion of a body along a circular path is called circular motion.
The force that acts on a body moving in a circular path and is directed towards the center of the circle is called centripetal force.
The rate of change of angular displacement is called angular velocity.
The force of attraction between any two bodies in the universe is called gravitational force.
The gravitational force acting on a body is called its weight.

importantQuestions.question: Define centripetal force and give its formula.
importantQuestions.answer: The force that acts on a body moving in a circular path and is directed towards the center of the circle is called centripetal force. Formula: Fc = mv²/r

importantQuestions.question: State Newton's law of universal gravitation and derive the formula for gravitational force.
importantQuestions.answer: Newton's law of universal gravitation states that every particle in the universe attracts every other particle with a force that is directly proportional to the product of their masses and inversely proportional to the square of distance between them. Derivation: F ∝ m₁m₂ and F ∝ 1/r². Combining: F ∝ m₁m₂/r². Therefore: F = Gm₁m₂/r² where G is universal gravitational constant.

mcqs.question: The centripetal force for a satellite orbiting Earth is provided by:
If the radius of circular path is doubled, the centripetal force becomes:
The value of 'g' at Karachi is approximately:

commonMistakes: Students confuse centripetal force with centrifugal force - remember centripetal acts towards center
Always write the formula before solving numerical problems
Don't forget to mention direction when defining centripetal force
Students mix up mass and weight - mass is constant but weight varies with gravity
In circular motion problems, always convert rpm to rad/s when needed
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Derivation of gravitational force formula appears frequently in long questions
Practice numerical problems on centripetal force and orbital motion
Memorize exact definitions as they appear in STBB textbook
note_data.keyDiagram: Circular motion diagram showing a body moving in circular path with velocity vector tangent to circle and centripetal force vector pointing towards center. Also draw Earth-satellite system showing gravitational force providing centripetal force.
